lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Closing the Deal -- Confirming Agreements
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Natalie Viegas | Aula 14 -- Closing the Deal
phases: From the Table to the Deal

listenings.text: Hi Natalie, this is Tom Bradley. Great news after our negotiation last week. I spoke to my director this morning, and he confirmed everything. He said that the terms were perfect, and he told me that procurement would sign off on the contract by Friday. I asked him about the timeline, and he said that we could kick off the project in early March. He also mentioned that the paperwork was almost ready, so we are on the same page. I think we found a real win-win, didn't we? Let us finalize the last details on a short call. Could you tell me if Wednesday works for you? Thanks, and talk soon.

Hi, this is Natalie, recording a quick reflection after we closed the deal with Tom. It feels great. Last week the negotiation was tense, but we found common ground. Today Tom called and told me that his director had approved everything. He said that the terms were perfect and that procurement would sign off by Friday. I asked him when we could start, and he said that we would kick off in March. The lesson for me is clear. A deal is not real until you confirm it and finalize the paperwork. Going forward, I always repeat what the client said, so we stay on the same page. I reported every point back to my manager, and she said that I had handled the whole process very well.

extra_audio.text: First, both sides reach an agreement and shake hands on the main terms. Then you repeat the key points out loud, so everyone is on the same page. After that, you put the agreement in writing and finalize the contract. Next, the right people review and sign off on the paperwork. Finally, you confirm the start date and kick off the project.
